commit.message:
Add build/test metrics dashboard, fix conflict detector false positives, fix PM review contradictions

- BuildTestMetrics: new service with 18 named metrics (build.*, test.*, rework.*, commits.*)
- AgentStateStore: added GetAggregateMetricsAsync, GetMetricsByAgentAsync query helpers
- EngineerAgentBase: wired metrics recording at all decision points (build attempts,
  successes, failures, fix attempts, regenerations, blocked commits, test runs, rework)
- All engineer subclasses (Senior, Junior, Principal) + TestEngineerAgent: accept optional
  BuildTestMetrics parameter via constructor
- Metrics.razor: 4 new dashboard sections (Build Health, Test Health, Rework Health,
  Commit Quality) with success rate calculations and color-coded indicators
- Program.cs: registered BuildTestMetrics as singleton

Bug fixes:
- ConflictDetector: was comparing filenames only, causing false positive warnings when
  the same filename exists in different directories (e.g., src/Pages/Index.razor vs
  Pages/Index.razor). Now uses IsSuspiciousDuplicate() that checks path suffix overlap
  and parent directory similarity.
- PullRequestWorkflow.ApproveAndMaybeMergeAsync: reason parameter was ignored, approval
  comments now include the review rationale.
- ProgramManagerAgent + PrincipalEngineerAgent: AI response text contamination caused
  both CHANGES REQUESTED and APPROVED to appear in the same comment. Now strips standalone
  decision keyword lines from reviewBody before posting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent negative USD values in ScenarioEditor

Four defensive fixes for the %/USD mode toggle:

1. Disable USD/PLN toggle when no reference price — prevents toggling
   before stock data loads, which left percentage values in fixed-mode
   fields (root cause of negative dollar amounts and overflow).

2. Early return in toggle functions when currentPrice/Rate <= 0 —
   belt-and-suspenders guard matching the disabled button state.

3. Clamp toDelta output to >= -100% — a stock/FX can never drop more
   than 100%, so any input producing a delta below -100 is capped.
   This prevents impossible negative end values in calculations.

4. Add min=0 on fixed-mode inputs — browser validation hint that
   absolute prices/rates cannot be negative.

commit.message:
Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal (#15980)

* Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal

When 'aspire start' runs from the VS Code Aspire terminal, the detached
child process inherits ASPIRE_EXTENSION_* environment variables, causing
it to detect extension mode and delegate back to the extension via
StartDebugSessionAsync instead of launching the AppHost. This results in
an immediate exit with code 0.

Fix:
- Strip extension-related env vars from the detached child process in
  DetachedProcessLauncher (Unix: remove from startInfo.Environment,
  Windows: build filtered environment block for CreateProcessW)
- Add --non-interactive guard in RunCommand to skip extension delegation
  when running as a child of 'aspire start'
- Set isExtensionHost=false in StartCommand so Dashboard URL always
  shows in 'aspire start' output
- Fix column width calculation in RenderAppHostSummary to only include
  labels that are actually displayed
- Add missing KnownConfigNames constants for extension env vars

Fixes #15786

* Fix detached child env filtering

Preserve DEBUG_SESSION_* values when the detached child CLI is launched from the VS Code Aspire terminal so the AppHost keeps IDE execution and dashboard debug integration. Also address follow-up review issues in StartCommand and the Windows filtered environment block builder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: prefix-based env var filter, remove unused usings

* Restore using Aspire.Cli.Utils needed for ICliUpdateNotifier

* Add context comment explaining why isExtensionHost is always false

---------

fix: mode mismatch when switching models in USD/PLN display

When ScenarioEditor was in fixed-value mode (USD/PLN) and the user
switched prediction models, the useEffect that syncs localValues from
the new scenarios always wrote raw percentage deltas regardless of
display mode.  This caused percentage values (e.g. -15) to appear as
USD prices, and toDelta() to compute deltas like -107.5%, producing
nonsensical results.

The fix converts percentage deltas to the current display mode (absolute
USD/PLN prices) when syncing from an external scenarios change, using
the same conversion logic as toggleStockMode/toggleFxMode.

commit.message:
feat: Lint rule to detect ordering discrepancies on top level functions (#8996)

Closes #8977

The following notebook:

```python
import marimo

__generated_with = "unknown"
app = marimo.App(width="medium")

with app.setup:
    from dataclasses import dataclass


@app.cell
def _():
    # UI marked as reuable by ui but file cell decorator is not changing UNLESS moved to after referenced dataclasses ** 
    @dataclass
    class ParsedFile:
        "A parsed notebook file"
        imports: list[Const]
    return


@app.class_definition
@dataclass
class Const:
    "A constant assignment from a setup cell."
    name: str               # variable name
    src: str


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run()
```

Cannot serialize `ParsedFile` as a top level function because it depends
on `Const`. This is not well captured in the frontend and can lead to
user frustration and confusion. This lint rule is added to help mitigate
this footgun by suggesting a reordering. e.g.

```txt
error[reusable-definition-order]: Reusable definition 'uses_offset' depends on reusable definition(s) declared later in the notebook: `offset`
 --> tests/_lint/test_files/reusable_definition_order.py:7:1
   7 | @app.function
   8 | def uses_offset(x: int = offset()) -> int:
     |     ^
   9 |     return x + 1
hint: Move the referenced reusable definition(s) earlier in the notebook, before `uses_offset`.
```

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve telemetry robustness and fix Linux device ID (#2405)

## Describe your changes

### Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS
- Remove erroneous `@property` decorator on module-level
`get_telemetry_base_dir()` in `utils.py`. This turned the function into
a non-callable property descriptor, silently breaking device ID
retrieval on all Linux/macOS systems (Windows uses the registry and was
unaffected).
- Add graceful fallbacks when `HOME` is unset (common in containers):
tries `Path.home()`, then `/var/tmp`.
- Simplify `_store.py` with `mkdir(exist_ok=True)` to avoid race
conditions.

### Auto-disable telemetry in CI/CD environments
- Detect well-known CI env vars (`CI`, `TF_BUILD`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS`,
`JENKINS_URL`, `CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID`, `BUILDKITE`,
`SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI`) and auto-disable telemetry.
- CI check runs **before** heartbeat so no events are sent at all in
pipelines.
- User opt-out via `OLIVE_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1` runs **after** heartbeat
(intentional: heartbeat still fires for user telemetry).

### Fix robustness issues
- **Cache entries silently lost**: Add missing `json.loads()` after
base64 decode in `_read_cache_entries()` — without this, cached events
were always dropped on retry.
- **Crash when logger is None**: Guard all `self._logger` call sites
with None checks.
- **File handle leak**: Close file handle if lock acquisition fails in
`_ExclusiveFileLock`.
- **Negative HTTP timeout**: Clamp deadline calculation to `max(0.1,
...)` in exporter.
- **Session leak on init failure**: Close HTTP session if exporter init
fails post-session creation.
- **Transport retry**: Catch `RequestException` (not just
`ConnectionError`) on retry.
- **Cache flush race**: Use `wait_for_callbacks()` instead of single
snapshot check.
- **Crash-proof public API**: Wrap all public `Telemetry` methods in
`try/except`.

### Other fixes
- Fix `docs/Privacy.md`: correct `OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_PATH` →
`OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_DIR`, document as directory path, add CI
auto-detection docs.
- Add `# noqa: S110` comments to satisfy CodeQL empty-except findings.
- Exporter validates `options is not None` with clear error message.

## Checklist before requesting a review
- [ ] Add unit tests for this change.
- [x] Make sure all tests can pass.
- [x] Update documents if necessary.
- [x] Lint and apply fixes to your code by running `lintrunner -a`
- [x] Is this a user-facing change? If yes, give a description of this
change to be included in the release notes.

Description of change for release notes:
> Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS, auto-disable telemetry in
CI/CD environments, and fix multiple robustness issues in the telemetry
cache.

---------

commit.message:
[a11y] Fix `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules` violations across 55 kibana-presentation files (#260694)

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/260637

Resolves 88 ESLint violations of `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules`
across 55 files owned by `@elastic/kibana-presentation`. Every `EuiIcon`
must have either an accessible label (`aria-label`, `title`, or
`aria-labelledby`) or be marked `aria-hidden={true}` if decorative.

## Strategy

**`aria-hidden={true}`** — applied to the majority of icons that are
decorative: drag handles inside labeled containers, icons in context
menu items (where the `name` field provides the label), icons alongside
visible text, and status icons inside tooltipped wrappers.

```tsx
// before
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" />

// after — parent div already carries aria-label
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" aria-hidden={true} />
```

**`aria-label` via `i18n.translate()`** — applied to icons that
independently convey meaning or are interactive:

| File | Icon | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `presentation_panel_title.tsx` | `info` with `tabIndex={0}` |
`aria-label="Description"` |
| `error_component.tsx` | `warning` with `onClick` | `aria-label="View
error details"` |
| `asset_picker.tsx` | `checkInCircleFilled` (selected state) |
`aria-label="Selected"` |
| `saved_query_management_list.tsx` | `info` / `clock` metadata
indicators | `aria-label="Has description"` / `aria-label="Has time
filter"` |

**`aria-label` on parent + `aria-hidden` on icon** —
`page_manager.component.tsx` had an unlabeled native `<button>` whose
only content was an icon; added `aria-label` to the button and
`aria-hidden={true}` to the icon.

---------

fix: align DepthRenderer readiness check with rendering logic (#18266)

## Problem

`DepthRenderer.customIsReadyFunction` checks `isReady()` for all
submeshes, but `DepthRenderer.isReady()` returns `false` for materials
with `disableDepthWrite`. The actual `renderSubMesh` function skips
those submeshes entirely. This mismatch causes `scene.whenReadyAsync()`
to never resolve when any mesh in the scene has `disableDepthWrite =
true`.

Forum report: https://forum.babylonjs.com/t/63104

## Fix

Added the same skip conditions from `renderSubMesh` (no material,
`infiniteDistance`, `disableDepthWrite`, zero vertices) as `continue`
guards in `customIsReadyFunction`.

## Tests

Added unit tests in
`packages/dev/core/test/unit/Rendering/babylon.depthRenderer.test.ts`.

commit.message:
Fix json_quote dropping ByteConcat tail when quoting (#7)

## Problem

`json_quote` silently truncates regexes built from string literals
longer than 31 bytes (`MAX_BYTE_CONCAT`) when those literals contain
characters that need JSON escaping (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, `\\\\`, `"`,
control chars, `\x7F`).

Long literals are internally split into chained `ByteConcat(bytes,
tail)` nodes. When `json_quote` encounters a chunk whose bytes include a
character needing quoting, it splits and re-encodes those bytes — but
drops `args[0]` (the mapped tail), discarding everything after that
chunk.

The `else` branches correctly preserve the tail; only the quoting branch
was missing it.

Reported in guidance-ai/llguidance#326.

## Fix

Append `args[0]` (the mapped tail expression) to `acc` before calling
`_mk_concat_vec`, matching the behavior of the other branches.

## Test

Added `test_json_quote_long_literal_with_newline` — a 78-byte literal
with `\n` at byte offset 43. Before the fix, the json-quoted regex fails
to match the correctly escaped output. After the fix, it matches.

All existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
